response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings genuine technical depth across a wide stack and a strong open-source commitment, but fails on two near-disqualifying criteria. Most critically, the role requires German at C1 level for client-facing consulting work — the resume explicitly lists German as 'Basic,' which is incompatible with the position's communication demands. Beyond the language gap, the profile is consistently that of a senior individual contributor: there is no demonstrated track record of team leadership, client advisory responsibility, project management governance, or architectural decision-making at an organizational level. UML expertise and Python — both listed as required or desired — are entirely absent from the resume.
strengths: 15+ years of hands-on development spanning embedded systems, high-throughput backend services, mobile, and full-stack web — genuine technical breadth across diverse real-world contexts
Strong open-source commitment with self-initiated, sustained projects (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and a public conference talk at FOSS4G 2019
Demonstrated culture of knowledge transfer through organizing Refactoring Dojos, Code Kata sessions, a Selenium course, and full-day public workshops
concerns: German proficiency self-assessed as 'Basic,' far below the C1 minimum required for client advising and executive collaboration in a German-speaking consulting context — this is likely a hard blocker
No evidence of team leadership, headcount responsibility, or management at any level; every listed role is an individual contributor position
UML, formal architecture methodologies, requirements management frameworks, IT security, and risk/compliance experience are all explicitly required by the JD and entirely absent from the resume

key_factors.factor: German language gap (Basic vs. C1 required)
No leadership or management track record
Broad open-source and multi-domain engineering experience
